source: City of Oceanside ArcGIS Feature Service: CIP_Engineering_AGOL (Points, Polygons, Lines layers)
coverage: Engineering CIP — active + recently completed. Earliest begin_year in current feed: 2021. Does NOT include Water Utilities projects and does NOT include pre-2021 historical projects.
known_gaps: cost_usd is null for every row — not published in the public GIS feed.
20-year history is not covered by this feed (see context.md for backfill plan).
Water Utilities capital projects are a separate program (not in this feed).
Schedule is encoded as season + year (e.g., WINTER 2024 -> SUMMER 2025), not exact dates.

projects.name: Cleveland Storm Drain
projects.description: This project provides for the installation of new storm drain facilities and replacement of failling corrugated metal pipes.
projects.recent_update: Project is currently working towards receiving a Coastal Development Permit.

projects.name: Full Capture Devices R2
projects.description: City-wide
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Sleeping Indian & North River Road
projects.description: This project provides for design of storm drain upgrades at five points dischage in the tnersections of North River Roda and Sleeping Indian Road
projects.location: Intersection of Sleeping Indian Road and North River Road
projects.recent_update: Awaiting Environmental Clearance for the proposed project.

projects.name: South Nevada Street Drainage Improvements
projects.description: This project provides for the design of drainage improvements in South Nevada Street and a drainage redirection through CAssidy Street.
projects.location: South Nevada Street and Cassidy Street
projects.recent_update: 100% plans to be submitted to SDG&E for final review

projects.name: Strawberry Place and El Camino Storm Drain Repair
projects.description: Strawberry Place storm drain repair Storm Drain Repair is a slip lining project designed to repair a failing corrugated metal storm drain pipe.
projects.recent_update: Strawberry Place and El Camino Real construction is complete. Project is in close-out.

projects.name: Full Trash Capture Devices Round 3
projects.description: This project provides for the 3rd round of trash capture device installation as mandated by state laws.
projects.recent_update: Round 3 installation is complete

projects.name: Buena Vista Weir
projects.description: This project allowed for steel plates to be placed along the face of the timber weir after planks were broken which resulted in the lagoon to be partially flushed into the Pacific Ocean. The project will also seek a "more permanent" solution.
projects.recent_update: Making CEQA Determination and submitting permit applications to CCC, Water Board, USACE, and CDFW for the "more permanent" solution highlighted above.

projects.name: Fire Station No. 1
projects.description: 410 Freeman St

projects.name: Pier View Way Bridge and Lifeguard Headquarters Project
projects.description: This project provides for preliminary design, environmental clearance and permitting for the reconstruction of the concrete bridge connecting Pacific Street to the Pier. This project also provides for the reconstruction of the Lifeguard Headquarters and a new Lifeguard Service Facility on the east side of the Strand.
projects.recent_update: The project is in the Environmental and Preliminary Design Phase. This preliminary phase is expected to be complete in Summer 2026.

projects.name: OPD HQ Expansion Relocation Study
projects.description: This project provides for completion of a study to determine the necessary expansion(s) and assess future, long term relocation options for the Oceanside Police Department Headquarters. The Oceanside Police Department Improvements project includes improvements to the evidence room and property storage at the City Operation Center and the addition of a staff restroom.

projects.name: Fire Station No. 4 Phase II Remodel
projects.description: This project provides for the repair of fire station deficiencies, including roofing repairs, HVAC cleaning, ceiling tile replacement, flooring, and wall repairs.
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Beachfront Improvements Feasibility Study - Phase II
projects.description: This project provides for preliminary design, environmental clearance and permitting for the Beachfront Improvement Phase II area. The Phase II area includes the Junior Seau Beach Community Center, Amphitheater/Bandshell, Pier Plaza, and public sections located in between. This project will advance the conceptual renderings to more detailed architectural and engineering project plans.
projects.recent_update: The project is in the Environmental and Preliminary Design Phase. This phase is anticipated to be complete in Summer 2026.

projects.name: Crown Heights Rehab Phase II
projects.description: The project consists of the preparation of plans for the rehabilitation of the Crown Heights Community Resource Center Building. The proposed repairs consist of restroom repairs, roofing, plumbing, electrical, lighting, and fire suppression.
projects.recent_update: Construction is on-going.

projects.name: Code Enforcement - Phase 3 Trailer Remodel
projects.description: 3131 Oceanside Blvd

projects.name: Fire Station 8
projects.description: This project provides for the design of an approximately 19,000 SF fire station bounded by Old Grove Road to the north, Trestle Street to the west, Rocky Point Drive to the south, and the Front Wave Credit Union to the east. The project site is a share site with Recycled Water Reservoir
projects.recent_update: Working to finalize plans, receive approval from Building & Engineering Dept, and receive final plans from SDG&E

projects.name: Library Courtyard
projects.description: This project provides for improvements to the existing Civic Center Library Courtyard to better accommodate Library sponsored events.
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Brooks Street Swim Center Renovations
projects.description: The project consisted of renovating and re-plastering the Brooks Street Pool.
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Junior Lifeguard Headquarter and Restroom
projects.description: This project provides for project design, construction, and project closeout costs associated with the Junior Lifeguard Headquater and restroom replacement. The current Jr. Lifeguard Program operates from multiple facilities and it is necessary to consolidate the operations for better functionality and efficiency.
projects.recent_update: Project on hold. Awaiting direction regarding the Marine Safety Unit project.

projects.name: Various Gym Study & Preliminary Design
projects.description: The project provides for a study and preliminary design of El Corazon Civic Services Site 3
projects.recent_update: RFP is drafted.

projects.name: Fire Training Center Buildings
projects.description: This project provides for the replacement of modular buildings at the Fire Training Center with permanent buildings.
projects.recent_update: Working to underground utilities and determine a feasible concept plan.

projects.name: Oceanside Pier and Restaurant - Kiosk Demolition
projects.description: This project provides the demolition of the hammerhead portion of the Oceanside Pier and Restaurant-Kiosk to include over-water demolition and removal of fire damaged property.
projects.recent_update: Demolition of the fire-impacted areas is complete. Efforts to reconstruct the hammerhead of the Pier are in progress.

projects.name: Mission Branch Library Restrooms
projects.description: Public restrooms in Mission Branch Library to be remodeled following the library strategic plan 2024-2028
projects.recent_update: Project is in the 60% design phase.

projects.name: Marine Safety Unit Modular Unit (formerly Harbor Renovations)
projects.description: Remodel/renovations to the Harbor HQ building located at 1540 Harbor Drive to accommodate Marine Safety Unit (MSU) staff and their 24/7 housing needs. May also require TI's to Harbor staff area to accommodate staff areas being relocated.
projects.recent_update: Preparing Request for Proposal to on-board design consultant

projects.name: Fire Mountain Undergrounding Project (Rule 20B)
projects.description: A combined coordination of overhead utilities to be placed underground from Brichwood to Via Esmarcaalong Fire Mountain Rd
projects.location: Fire Mountain Dr from Birchwood Way to and Via Esmarca

projects.name: Quiet Zone Re-Certification
projects.description: This project will re-certify the Downtown Oceanside railroad quiet zone to maintain compliance with Federal Railroad Administration requirements.
projects.location: Surfrider Way, Mission Ave, Wisconsin Ave, Oceanside Blvd and Cassidy Street
projects.recent_update: Working with consultant on document review and field conditions assessment

projects.name: Oceanside Blvd & Crouch St Undergrounding Project (Rule 20 A)
projects.description: A combined coordination of overhead utilities to be placedunderground from rom Greenbrier to Crouch Street along Oceanside Blvd.
projects.location: From Greenbrierto Crouch Street along Oceanside Blvd.
projects.recent_update: Project is in design with SDG&E

projects.name: Street Market Bollards
projects.description: This project provides funding for the purchase of barriers for the Downtown markets (Morning Farmer's Market and Sunset Market).
projects.location: Multiple locations at Farmers Market and Sunset Market Street Entrances and Intersections.
projects.recent_update: Barriers have been purchased.

projects.name: Tyson Bluff Design
projects.description: This project provides for design of improvements to repair and stabilize the Tyson Bluff slope behind Tyson Street Park.
projects.location: Behind Tyson Street Park, along S Pacific St
projects.recent_update: Working on getting support from Coastal Commission on project

projects.name: Olive Drive Guardrail
projects.description: This project provides street improvements along Olive Drive including the construction of a steel midwest guardrail system.
projects.recent_update: Project is anticipated to start construction in October 2025 and be complete by November 2025

projects.name: Northerly Street Slope Repair
projects.description: This project consists of new storm drain piping and repair of a slope failure.
projects.recent_update: Blue Pacific received a Notice to Proceed on October 21st, 2024.

projects.name: Inland Rail Trail Feasibility Study
projects.description: The project will evaluate the alignment of the Inland Rail TRail along the North County Transit District (NCTD) Sprinter Line from Melrose Drive to the Costal Rail Trail.
projects.location: Between Melrose Drive to the Costal Rail Trail.
projects.recent_update: Feasibility Study is set to be complete by April 2026.

projects.name: Crown Heights Pocket Park Improvements
projects.description: The Pocket Park is to be prepared for installment of a single modular unit. The modular will be a temporary location that will provide various services for the community during the remodel of the Crown heights Community Center.
projects.location: Northeast corner of Brooks Street and Division Street
projects.recent_update: Site is currently the home of the trailer complex for the Cesar Chavez Resource Center while construction is in place.

projects.name: Buccaneer Park Improvements
projects.description: This Measure X project provides for improvements to Buccaneer Park including a new restroom and new cafe building. https://buccaneer-park-ktuagis.hub.arcgis.com/
projects.recent_update: Staff received approval of the concept design in March 2025. Staff is working on advancing the design.

projects.name: Calavera Creek Crossing
projects.description: The project provides for design of a crossing at Flood Structure #4 within Calavera Creek adjacent to Oak Riparian Park to allow acess to the Calavera Trail System.
projects.recent_update: Determining scope of work based on findings.

projects.name: Joe Balderamma Park Restroom
projects.description: This project provides for the design of a new restroom at Joe Balderamma Park.
projects.recent_update: Developing Request for Proposal for Design Services of the Restroom remodel.

projects.name: El Corazon Park Site 1
projects.description: This project provides for design and construction of a park at El Corazon, known as Park Site 1, which is approximately seventeen (17) acres. Park Site 1 is the first of nine parks scheduled for design and construction per the El Corazon Specific Plan.
projects.recent_update: Design is at 90%. Staff is working on refinements of the design and finalizing design of the pump track.

projects.name: John Landes Park Improvements
projects.description: The project provides for City Match Funds for the installation of new skate elements, lighting, and shade structures at John Landes Park.
projects.recent_update: Construction on the new skate spot elements has completed.

projects.name: Ron Ortega Dog Facility
projects.description: The Ron Ortega Dog Facility scope includes development of up to 2.8 acres of vacant, unprogrammed, and under-utilized space designated in the diagram below. It is anticipated to include separate off-leash, fenced pens for large and small dogs to run and play freely, and will also include seating, shade structure(s), and drinking fountains for both dogs and humans. Improvements located outside of the fenced pens include lit, accessible sidewalks, as well as landscape and drainage improvements.
projects.recent_update: Preliminary design currently underway.

projects.name: Cesar Chavez Park Improvements
projects.description: The project provides for new playground & fencing at the Cesar Chavez Park in the Crown Heights community.
projects.recent_update: Construction of playground was complete October 2023. Construction of fence was complete June 2024

projects.name: City-wide Playground Replacements: MLK Playground
projects.description: The project provides for replacement of 17 play structures throughout the City, including sites located at Martin Luther King Park, South Pier Park, Tyson Street Park, Marshall Street Park, Palisades Park, Buddy Todd Park, Capistrano Park, Libby Lake Park, South Oceanside Park, Melba Bishop Park, John Landes Park, Oak Riparian Park, Fireside Park, Alamosa Park, Spring Creek Park, Luiseno Park and Mance Buchanon Park. The 17 play structures were originally installed between 1998 and 2006.
projects.recent_update: MLK Park - Project to be approved for Construction on June 26th.

projects.name: Libby Lake Lighting & Futsal Court
projects.description: This CDBG project provides for the installation of additional lighting, demolition of existing volleyball court, and installation of a mini-pitch (futsal) court. Project is a CDBG project and is teamed up with the US Soccer Foundation to construct mini-pitch court.
projects.recent_update: The project is complete. The courts opened to the public on January 28, 2025

projects.name: El Corazon Phase II Trails
projects.description: This project provides for the design and construction of trails in El Corazon
projects.recent_update: Project is anticipated to start construction in October.

projects.name: John Landes EDI Grant
projects.description: The City received $1M to provide updates to John Landes Park.
projects.recent_update: Project on hold due to funding availability.

projects.name: Ron Ortega Little League Upgrades
projects.description: The project is to upgrade the Little League baseball field and address conditions of the park restrooms.
projects.recent_update: Awaiting direction for long-term improvements. Short-term improvements to be coordinated with Public Works.

projects.name: John Landes Park Lighting
projects.description: Project will either replace existing SDG&E light fixtures or install additional lights to illuminate the field in John Landes Park.
projects.recent_update: SDG&E replaced existing lighting in September 2025

projects.name: Prince Memorial Skatepark Lighting
projects.description: Project will install solar lights to illuminate the skatepark
projects.recent_update: Procuring contract for solar lighting design and installation.

projects.name: Fireside Park Expansion
projects.description: Project will in-fill recreation areas and construct a new restroom
projects.recent_update: Project preparing outreach material

projects.name: Sepulveda Park Expansion
projects.description: Project will in-fill recreation areas
projects.recent_update: Project preparing for Public Outreach

projects.name: Douglas Bridge Seismic Retrofit
projects.description: This federally funded project provides for the retrofit of Douglas Drive Bridge (over the San Luis Rey River) in order to meet earthquake standards.
projects.location: Douglas Dr between Old River St & Pala Rd
projects.recent_update: All regulatory agency permits have been submitted as of December 2024. Staff is working with the various regulatory agencies to get permit approval.

projects.name: Coast Highway Bridge Replacement
projects.description: This project provides for demolition and replacement of the existing 947-foot, 7-span, concrete deck/steel girder composite structure with a new two-lane structure.
projects.recent_update: Project design is currently at 90%

projects.name: South Strand Study
projects.description: This is two phased project; The first phase of the project included interim repairs and AC overlay as a stop gap to address some of the current road deterioration. The second phase of the project includes a study to evaluate the existing road conditions, including any voids and seawall, and develop future design alternatives to stabilize and address the long term needs of this roadway.
projects.recent_update: Phase 1 is completed. The study is in the PSR phase. A RFP is being prepared.

projects.name: Thunder Drive Road Rehabilitation
projects.description: Work completed as an part of annual overlay project. no anticipated work or funding needed this fiscal year.
projects.location: Thunder Drive from Plaza Dr to Bonita Drive
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Annual Road Repairs: Asphalt Overlay (FY22-23)
projects.description: Roadway Repair - Overlay for City streets based on the approved Pavement Management Program (PMP). Work is also planned in coordination with other projects and programs, public requests, and staff recommendations. Provides additional funding to support the City's efforts to maintain the City's roadway system.
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Grandview Street Improvements
projects.location: Grandview St East of Cole Way
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: BV Audubon Crosswalk Study
projects.description: This project provides for a study to properly locate a crosswalk, complete with rapid flashing beacons, to allow for safe crossing by the public from the east side of Coast Highway to the west side at near the Buena Vista Lagoon.
projects.recent_update: The project is in the PSR phase

projects.name: McNeil Street Improvements
projects.description: The project provides street improvements along McNeil Street including the installation of improved drainage features, striping and signage, Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) ramps, new sidewalk and widening the easterly portion of the street.
projects.recent_update: Construction is set to be completed by May 2026.

projects.name: Libby Lake ADA Pedestrian Ramps
projects.description: The project provides for new sidewalk , cub & gutter, cross gutter and ADA pedstrian ramps in the Libby Lake community.
projects.location: Calle Montecito, Calle Vallecito, Calle Las Positas and Casa Drive
projects.recent_update: Construction and close-out are complete.

projects.name: Vista Way Overlay
projects.description: Project will address road damage due to SR 78 detour. The project includes a 2 inch grind and overlay of Vista Way from El Camino Real and College Blvd.
projects.location: Vista Way from El Camino Real to College Blvd.
projects.recent_update: Project design is currently at 100%. Coordinating with Caltrans to receive authorization to proceed with construction.

projects.name: Annual Road Repairs: Asphalt Overlay & Slurry Seal (FY23-24)
projects.description: Annual Roadway Repair for FY23-24 based on the approved Pavement Management Program (PMP). This project includes traffic calming measures as well.
projects.recent_update: Public Works Agreement awarded to ATP Engineering Contractors in June of 2024. Roadway repairs began August of 2024.

projects.name: ADA Ramp Replacement Infill 24-25
projects.description: This project includes the installation of ADA compliant pedestrain ramps and infill of sidewalk.
projects.recent_update: Project construction is complete.

projects.name: Saratoga Street Driveway
projects.description: Re-construction of the driveway, sidewalk and curb & gutter along the frontage of 2418 and 2420 Saratoga Street.
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Cassidy Street Pedestrian Bridge
projects.description: The concrete bridge structure that spans Cassidy Street between Myers Street and Pacific Street was constructed in 1927, the same year as the concrete portion of the Oceanside Pier. The bridge was closed to vehicle traffic in response to pieces of the bridge separating and falling onto Cassidy Street. The bridge currently serves as a pedestrian only foot bridge and bicycle route.
projects.location: Between Pacific Street and Myers Street

projects.name: San Rafael Drive Sidewalk Infill
projects.description: Sidewalk Improvements to be added along San Rafael Drive
projects.location: Between San Rafael Drive and Sunset Drive
projects.recent_update: Project is complete.

projects.name: Mesa Dr. (HSIP) ADA Pedestrian Ramps
projects.description: Project will upgrade the existing non-compliant pedestrian ramps at the Mesa Dr. and Avenida De La Plata
projects.location: Mesa Dr. and Avenida De La Plata
projects.recent_update: Project is complete.

projects.name: 485 Vandergrift Blvd (Marietas) Improvements
projects.description: Project will remove and replace damaged sidewalk and driveway. ADA improvements will be included.
projects.recent_update: Project construction was completed on July 25, 2025

projects.name: Canyon/Landfill Bridge
projects.description: This project aims to fix the settlement of the approach slab at the Canyon Landfill Bridge.
projects.recent_update: Project in planning and design. Funding to be determined.

projects.name: Oceanside Blvd & El Camino Real Barrier
projects.description: The project will install a traffic safety barrier at the northeast corner of Oceanside Blvd and El Camino Real, south of the Quarry.
projects.location: Northeast corner of Oceanside Blvd and El Camino Real, south of the Quarry.

projects.name: Coastal Rail Trail: Oceanside to Morse Street
projects.description: This project provides for the design of the missing of the Coastal Rail Trail between Oceanside Blvd to Morse Street.
projects.location: Along rail tracks from Oceanside Blvd to Morse Street
projects.recent_update: Project is currently at 60% design. Currently working towards getting a Coastal Development Permit.

projects.name: College Boulevard Widening
projects.description: The project will widen College Blvd from Olive Drive to Old Grove Road and inlcudes bicycle and pedetrian improvements with a Class IV Cycle track, bicycle signals, separated pedestrian walkways, raised crosswalks, and landscaping.
projects.location: College Boulevard between Waring Rd & Old Grove Rd
projects.recent_update: The project is in the design phase with 60% plans anticipated in Fall.

projects.name: HSIP Traffic Signal Upgrades
projects.description: The project provides traffic signal upgrades at various corridors including the installation audible pedestrian countdown signal heads, ADA compliant audible pedestrian pushbutton system, and upgrading traffic signal controllers at various locations.
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Laurel Elementary Safe Route to Schools ATP
projects.description: The project provides street improvements along portions of Laurel Street, Holly Street and San Diego Street including the installation of high visibility crosswalks, flashing beacons, striping and signage, a traffic calming median, a traffic circle, a mini-roundabout, ADA ramps, new sidewalk, and curb extensions.
projects.location: Intersection of Laurel Street and Holly Street
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Coast Highway Corridor Design
projects.description: This project will create a "Complete Street" along a section of Coast Highway from Neptune Way, south to Oceanside Boulevard.
projects.location: South Coast Highway from Neptune Way to Oceanside Boulevard.
projects.recent_update: Currently at a 90% design level

projects.name: Three Traffic Signals
projects.description: The project provides for traffic signal modifications at three (3) locations. The locations are Coast Highway and Seagaze Drive; North River Road and Douglas Drive; Foussat Road and Oceanside Blvd.
projects.location: Coast Highway and Seagaze Drive; North River Road and Douglas Drive; Foussat Road and Oceanside Blvd
projects.recent_update: Construction is complete.

projects.name: Rancho Del Oro/Mesa Drive Traffic Signal
projects.description: This project provides for the construction of a new traffic signalized intersection including the replacement of existing traffic signal poles, replace existing rigid conduit that is failing and construct new ADA ramps at this intersection.the construction of a new traffic signalized intersection including the replacement of existing traffic signal poles, replace existing rigid conduit that is failing and construct new ADA ramps at this intersection.
projects.location: Intersection @ Mesa Dr & Rancho del Oro Dr
projects.recent_update: Construction is set to begin by Fall 2026.

projects.name: Carey Road/Canyon Drive Traffic Signal
projects.description: The project provides for the construction of a new traffic signalized intersection including the install of new traffic signal poles, conduit and ADA ramps at this intersection.
projects.location: Carey Road and Canyon Drive Intersection
projects.recent_update: Construction is set to begin by December 2026.
